steps.title: Identify the causal claim
steps.content: The student infers a causal relationship: similarity drives cross-category purchasing. The passage shows similar product pairs have higher cross-brand loyalty than dissimilar ones.

steps.title: Find the competing explanation
steps.content: Choice C presents an alternative explanation: high-similarity pairs cluster in low-competition markets, so lack of alternatives—not similarity itself—drives cross-category loyalty. This directly weakens the student's causal claim by showing another mechanism could explain the pattern. The other choices either restate the original finding, suggest a related factor without challenging the claim, or demonstrate the relationship holds across conditions. That matches choice C — C is correct.
